platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **RAG Engine Serverless Mode** (April 3, public preview): Fully managed database mode for RAG Engine that abstracts away database provisioning and scaling. Introduces new `RagManagedVertexVectorSearch` vector database type. Supports seamless transition between Serverless and Spanner deployment modes. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/rag-engine/serverless-mode

**Veo 3.1 Lite** (April 2, public preview): Most cost-efficient Veo video generation model on Vertex AI. Completes the Veo 3.1 family (standard, Fast, Lite) — all with native audio generation. Source: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/veo-3-1-lite-and-a-new-veo-upscaling-capability-on-vertex-ai

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Codex Plugin for Claude Code** (April 4): Official OpenAI MCP integration (`openai/codex-plugin-cc`) that allows Claude Code users to delegate code review, adversarial review, and background tasks to OpenAI Codex. Creates cross-provider data flow via Model Context Protocol. Source: https://github.com/openai/codex-plugin-cc

**Codex Pay-as-You-Go Team Seats** (April 2): ChatGPT Business and Enterprise workspaces can now add Codex-only seats with usage-based pricing (no fixed seat fee). Introduces a new user seat type within enterprise workspaces. Source: https://openai.com/index/codex-flexible-pricing-for-teams/

**ChatGPT in Apple CarPlay** (April 4, all plans): Voice-only ChatGPT access via Apple CarPlay on iOS 26.4+. Available on all plans including enterprise. New access surface for enterprise conversations. Source: https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001153-using-chatgpt-on-carplay

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Cursor 3 Interface** (April 2): Major release introducing three new capabilities:
**Agents Window**: Standalone workspace for running parallel agents across local, cloud, and remote SSH environments
**Design Mode**: Annotate and target UI elements directly in browsers for agent feedback
**Agent Tabs**: Side-by-side or grid view of multiple agent sessions
New `/worktree` command for isolated git worktrees, `/best-of-n` for parallel multi-model comparison, `Await` tool for monitoring long-running jobs, structured MCP content support
Source: https://cursor.com/changelog/3-0

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **v1.25.0** (April 2): Split-screen multitasking (drag tab to screen edge), custom tab icons (emoji favicons), Spotify integration visual (animated notes, hover for current track), download guidance animations. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

findings.title: Vertex AI RAG Engine Serverless Mode
findings.description: New fully managed deployment mode for RAG Engine in public preview. Introduces `RagManagedVertexVectorSearch` as a new vector database entity type. AISPM should discover RAG Engine deployment mode configurations and map Serverless vs Spanner resource types to track which mode tenant data resides in

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Plugin for Claude Code (MCP)
findings.description: Official cross-provider MCP integration allowing Claude Code to route code review and task delegation to OpenAI Codex. Creates a new enterprise data flow where source code and review context traverse from Anthropic's agent to OpenAI's Codex. Endpoint monitoring must detect this MCP server installation and flag the cross-provider data exfiltration surface

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Pay-as-You-Go Team Seats
findings.description: New Codex-only seat type in ChatGPT Business/Enterprise workspaces with usage-based billing. Introduces a new user entity type (Codex-only member) that may have different permissions and access patterns than standard seats. AISPM inventory should distinguish Codex-only seats from full ChatGPT seats when mapping workspace members

findings.title: ChatGPT CarPlay Voice Access
findings.description: ChatGPT now accessible via Apple CarPlay on all plans including enterprise. Creates a new unmonitored access surface — voice conversations in vehicles bypass typical enterprise DLP controls. AIDR should flag CarPlay as an activity source for enterprise session tracking

findings.title: Cursor 3 Agents Window and Parallel Execution
findings.description: Cursor 3 introduces a fundamentally new agent execution model — the Agents Window supports parallel agent execution across local, cloud, and remote SSH environments simultaneously. Design Mode adds browser-annotation capabilities. The `/worktree` command creates isolated git worktrees, and the `Await` tool monitors long-running subagents. Endpoint monitoring must account for parallel agent sessions, cloud-local handoff, and the expanded MCP structured content surface

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
details: **(Preview) Work IQ tools integration (March 2026)**: Connect Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents to the Work IQ service for real-time work insights and context from Microsoft 365 files, emails, meetings, and chats. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/use-work-iq

**Claude Sonnet 4.5 for Computer Use agents (February 2026)**: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (beta) is now selectable for Computer Use agents, improving nuanced decision-making for complex tasks. Source: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot/blog/copilot-studio/computer-use-is-now-in-public-preview-in-microsoft-copilot-studio/

**Prompt builder — Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.5 model selection (February 2026)**: Fine-grained control over reasoning depth, quality, latency, and cost per prompt via model selection in prompt builder. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/prompt-model-settings

**Prompt builder — per-prompt content moderation (February 2026)**: Configure content moderation sensitivity (low/high) per prompt for hate/fairness, sexual, violence, and self-harm content. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/prompt-model-settings

**Prompt builder — inline editing (February 2026)**: Edit prompt instructions and settings inline in agent tool details with model selection, inputs, knowledge, and testing in one view. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/prompt-model-settings

**Improved ticket-based M365 Graph connector responses (February 2026)**: Better retrieval of ServiceNow tickets and Azure DevOps work items with actionable summaries. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Codex v0.118.0 (March 31, 2026)**: Windows sandbox enforces proxy-only networking with OS-level egress rules; app-server clients support device code flow for ChatGPT sign-in; new `codex exec` command enables prompt-plus-stdin workflow; custom model providers can fetch/refresh short-lived bearer tokens dynamically. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog

**Codex v0.117.0 (March 26, 2026)**: Plugins are now a first-class workflow with startup sync and TUI browsing; sub-agents use path-based addresses (`/root/agent_a`); app-server clients support shell commands, filesystem watches, remote websocket connections; `view_image` returns image URLs; legacy artifact tool removed and `read_file`/`grep_files` deprecated; app-server-backed TUI enabled by default. Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/changelog

findings.title: Copilot Studio Work IQ Integration
findings.description: New Work IQ service integration (preview) enables Copilot Studio agents to access real-time work insights from Microsoft 365 files, emails, meetings, and chats. Introduces new entity types: Work IQ-connected agents, work insight data connections, and M365 context bindings. AISPM must discover agents with Work IQ enabled and map their data access patterns — these agents gain broad read access to organizational work data across M365 services, creating potential data leakage vectors

findings.title: Copilot Studio Computer Use Expansion
findings.description: Computer Use agents in Copilot Studio gained expanded capabilities: new model support (Claude Sonnet 4.5 beta), built-in credentials management, Cloud PC pooling, and enhanced audit logging with session replay. Computer Use agents can control mouse/keyboard and interact with desktop applications. AISPM must inventory Computer Use agents, their model bindings, credential stores, and Cloud PC allocations. The session replay audit logging is a new data artifact that should be tracked

findings.title: Copilot Studio Computer Use Audit Logging and Session Replay
findings.description: Computer Use agents now generate enhanced audit logs with full session replay capability. This is a new activity data surface — every screen interaction, credential usage, and agent action during a Computer Use session is recorded. AIDR should ingest these audit logs and session replays to detect anomalous agent behavior, unauthorized credential usage, or data exfiltration through desktop application interactions

findings.title: Copilot Studio Computer Use with Claude Models
findings.description: Computer Use agents can now use Claude Sonnet 4.5 for decision-making and control desktop applications with mouse/keyboard. This is a significant attack surface: prompt injection through on-screen content could manipulate agent actions, and credential-enabled agents operating on Cloud PCs introduce privilege escalation risks. AIRT should test Computer Use agents for visual prompt injection, credential abuse, and unauthorized application access scenarios

findings.title: Copilot Studio Multi-Model Prompt Builder
findings.description: Copilot Studio prompt builder now supports Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.5 model selection alongside existing models. Introduces per-prompt content moderation sensitivity configuration (low/high) for multiple content categories. AISPM model inventory must be updated to track which prompts use which models, and the content moderation configuration per prompt represents a new governance surface — misconfigured moderation could allow harmful content generation

findings.title: Copilot Studio VS Code Extension (GA)
findings.description: The Copilot Studio extension for Visual Studio Code reached general availability. Developers can now build and manage Copilot Studio agents directly from VS Code. Endpoint monitoring should track this extension as a local AI development surface that connects to the Copilot Studio platform

findings.title: OpenAI Codex Sub-Agent Architecture and Plugin System
findings.description: Codex v0.117.0 introduced a first-class plugin system with startup sync, TUI browsing, and path-based sub-agent addressing (`/root/agent_a`). Codex v0.118.0 added `codex exec` command for prompt-plus-stdin workflows and dynamic bearer token management for custom model providers. The sub-agent architecture means Codex sessions can now spawn and coordinate multiple autonomous agents. Endpoint monitoring should track Codex plugin configurations, sub-agent spawning, and the new network egress enforcement on Windows

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Lyria 3 Audio Generation (Public Preview) — March 25, 2026**: Two new AI audio/music generation models added to Vertex AI:
`lyria-3-pro-preview` — generates up to 184 seconds of audio
`lyria-3-clip-preview` — generates up to 30 seconds of audio

findings.title: Vertex AI Lyria 3 Audio Generation Models
findings.description: Google added two new generative AI model endpoints (`lyria-3-pro-preview`, `lyria-3-clip-preview`) to Vertex AI in public preview. These expand the platform's generative surface area beyond text and image into audio/music generation. If enterprise agents or workflows can invoke these models, AISPM should discover and inventory Lyria 3 deployments and track data flows through audio generation endpoints

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry (now "Microsoft Foundry")
details: **New "Explore more Azure AI" section** added to landing page with cross-references to **"What are Foundry Tools?"** and **"Microsoft Agent Framework"** — indicating new services/concepts in the Foundry ecosystem. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-studio/

**Foundry IQ (preview)** — new knowledge-base-connected agent feature announced in March 2026 what's new. Connects knowledge bases to Foundry Agent Service. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry
**Fireworks models (preview)** — new third-party model provider integration on Microsoft Foundry. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry
**Hosted agent lifecycle management** — new articles for deploying and managing hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry
**Claude Code for Microsoft Foundry** — new integration enabling Claude Code configuration with Microsoft Foundry. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry

**Microsoft Agent Framework** — comprehensive agent orchestration framework with support for multiple providers (Foundry, Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon Bedrock, GitHub Copilot, Ollama) and A2A protocol. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/

platforms.name: Power Apps
details: **New "Vibe" AI copilot experience (preview)** — AI-native platform that builds complete solutions (data models, business logic, UI) from natural language. Available at vibe.powerapps.com. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/vibe/overview

**New "Create a code app"** capability — code-first app creation using React/Vue with Power Platform deployment via `pac code push`. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/developer/code-apps/overview
**New version 26033** rolling out (Make.PowerApps.com), first available Mar 25, 2026. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/released-versions/powerapps

platforms.name: Power Pages
details: **New "Enable agent in your site"** capability — embed AI agents directly into Power Pages sites with site-context awareness and web role-based visibility control. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-pages/getting-started/enable-agent
**New "Force Bing webmaster to index your site"** option added under AI and Copilot section. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-pages/

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **SKILL.md file support** (v1.9577.24, Mar 9) — custom skill definitions from `.windsurf/skills/` directory with supporting files. Source: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/skills
**Enterprise MDM-managed system-level skill definitions** — organizations can deploy managed skill sets via MDM policies. Source: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/skills
**Quota billing system** (v1.9577.43, Mar 19) — new daily/weekly usage metrics within the IDE. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **Bug Bounty Program** launched via HackerOne for responsible vulnerability disclosure. Source: https://diabrowser.com/security
**Expanded infrastructure security details** — role-based production access restrictions, encryption in transit and at rest, regular data collection audits. Source: https://diabrowser.com/security

findings.title: Microsoft Foundry IQ (Preview)
findings.description: New knowledge-base-connected agent feature in Foundry Agent Service. Foundry IQ enables agents to query connected knowledge bases, introducing new entity types: IQ-enabled agents, knowledge base connections, and IQ query sessions. AISPM must discover and inventory Foundry IQ agents and their knowledge base bindings to map data access patterns and ensure sensitive knowledge bases are governed

findings.title: Microsoft Agent Framework
findings.description: New comprehensive agent orchestration framework with support for 11+ inference providers (Foundry, Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon Bedrock, GitHub Copilot, Ollama) and A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol for cross-agent communication. Introduces new entity types: framework-managed agents, multi-provider agent configurations, A2A proxy agents, and hosted agent deployments. AISPM must discover agents built with this framework across all supported providers and map their tool bindings, MCP connections, and inter-agent communication patterns

findings.title: Power Apps "Vibe" AI Copilot Experience
findings.description: New AI-native app creation experience in Power Apps (preview). Users describe business problems in natural language, and Vibe generates complete solutions including data models, business logic, and UI code. Creates new entity types (Vibe-generated apps, AI-created data models and code components) that need discovery and inventory tracking. AISPM must map Vibe apps as a distinct resource type — they may contain AI-generated code with different security characteristics than manually built apps

findings.title: Power Pages Agent Enablement
findings.description: New capability to embed AI agents within Power Pages sites. Agents automatically gain site-context awareness and can generate responses based on visitor web roles. Introduces agent entities deployed on public-facing web pages with role-based access control. AISPM must discover these agents and AIDR must monitor their data flows given the external exposure surface — agents responding to unauthenticated or low-privilege visitors pose data leakage risk

findings.title: Microsoft Foundry Hosted Agents
findings.description: New managed deployment model for AI agents in Foundry Agent Service. Agents can be deployed as hosted services with lifecycle management (create, deploy, scale, stop). Introduces new infrastructure entities: hosted agent instances, deployment configurations, and service endpoints. AISPM must discover hosted agents and map their model bindings, tool access, and network exposure

findings.title: Fireworks Models on Microsoft Foundry (Preview)
findings.description: New third-party model provider (Fireworks AI) integrated into Microsoft Foundry. Expands the model catalog with Fireworks models. AISPM model inventory must be updated to discover and track deployments using Fireworks models for compliance and governance

findings.title: Foundry Tools (Rebranded Azure AI Services)
findings.description: "Foundry Tools" is the new name for Azure AI Services within the Microsoft Foundry platform. Includes Azure OpenAI, Content Safety, Speech, Vision, Document Intelligence, Language, and Translation services. While the underlying services exist, the rebranding under Foundry means new API endpoints, SDK paths, and resource identifiers that AISPM inventory mappings must be updated to track

findings.title: Power Apps Code Apps
findings.description: New code-first app creation type in Power Apps. Developers build with React/Vue locally and deploy to Power Platform via `pac code push`. These apps have different security characteristics than canvas/model-driven apps — developer-authored code runs within Power Platform's runtime. AISPM inventory should track this new app type distinctly

findings.title: Windsurf SKILL.md and Enterprise MDM Skills
findings.description: Windsurf added SKILL.md file support for custom skill definitions (`.windsurf/skills/`) and MDM-managed system-level skill definitions for Enterprise deployments. Skills can bundle supporting files and define multi-step workflows that Cascade AI executes. Enterprise MDM integration enables organization-wide skill policies deployed to all user machines — endpoint monitoring should track skill definitions and their tool/MCP interactions

findings.title: Claude Code Integration with Microsoft Foundry
findings.description: New documentation for configuring Claude Code as an AI development tool connected to Microsoft Foundry. This creates a new attack surface where Claude Code agents can interact with Foundry resources, models, and deployed agents. AIRT should test for prompt injection and privilege escalation in Claude Code sessions connected to Foundry
